packet.criterion_text: Objective severity trend: PASI, BSA, and/or IGA show a directional change across visits.

evidence.quote: PASI 6.1, BSA 8%, IGA 2 (mild-to-moderate plaque elevation and scale).
PASI 6.3 today (baseline 6.1 on 2026-02-02 — essentially flat, within measurement noise). BSA 8%. IGA 2.
PASI 14.2, BSA 15%, IGA 3. This is a marked change from 2026-03-16 (PASI 6.3) and baseline 2026-02-02 (PASI 6.1).
PASI 16.8, BSA 18%, IGA 3. DLQI 17. Compared with 2026-05-10 (PASI 14.2, DLQI 15), objective and quality-of-life measures are both worse.

packet.reasoning: Across four dated encounters, PASI rose from 6.1 (2026-02-02) to 6.3 (2026-03-16, essentially flat), then sharply to 14.2 (2026-05-10) and further to 16.8 (2026-06-29). This represents a PASI increase of approximately 10.7 points from baseline to the most recent visit, far exceeding the rubric threshold of ≥2 points. BSA likewise increased from 8% to 18%, and IGA stepped up from 2 to 3. The directional worsening is consistent and documented with verbatim quotes across all four visits.

packet.criterion_text: Symptom trajectory, including nail and joint involvement, shows a directional change.

evidence.quote: onycholysis noted on two fingernails (right index and right middle), with distal separation and slight yellowish discoloration

packet.reasoning: The rubric specifies that absence of mention is not evidence of absence. New nail involvement — onycholysis on two fingernails with distal separation and yellowish discoloration — is documented for the first time at the 2026-05-10 visit, representing a new symptom domain not present in earlier notes. This constitutes a directional worsening in symptom trajectory. No candidates were provided documenting nail or joint findings at earlier visits, consistent with these being new findings. The single candidate directly evidences new nail involvement.

packet.criterion_text: DLQI (Dermatology Life Quality Index) trend shows a directional change.

evidence.quote: Quality-of-life impact assessed with the Dermatology Life Quality Index: DLQI 7, driven by itch, clothing choices, and self-consciousness during exercise.
DLQI 8, reflecting ongoing nocturnal itch and mild sleep disturbance.
DLQI 15 today (was 8), with high scores on symptoms, feelings, and work domains.

packet.reasoning: DLQI scores across four visits: 7 (2026-02-02), 8 (2026-03-16), 15 (2026-05-10), and 17 (2026-06-29). The increase from 8 to 15 between visits 2 and 3 alone represents a 7-point rise, exceeding the rubric threshold of ≥4 points. The subsequent rise to 17 by visit 4 confirms a sustained upward trend. The total increase from baseline (7) to most recent visit (17) is 10 points. The directional worsening is unambiguous and documented with verbatim quotes across all four visits.

packet.criterion_text: Treatment-response adequacy: the current regimen has been given an adequate, adherent trial.

evidence.quote: doses taken 2026-05-10 and 2026-05-24; doses due 2026-06-07 and 2026-06-21 were missed. Net: two missed doses, i.e., no biologic received in the 5 weeks prior to today.
adherence and phototherapy attendance detail deferred to today's separate adherence review by clinic staff (filed separately)

packet.reasoning: The adherence note documents that two consecutive biologic doses (due 2026-06-07 and 2026-06-21) were missed, resulting in no biologic received in the 5 weeks prior to the most recent visit. The rubric specifies that missed doses or therapy gaps make adequacy unassessable. Because the current regimen was not taken as scheduled during the period in which the most significant worsening was observed, treatment-response adequacy cannot be established. The chart does not provide sufficient information about the full dosing history prior to the missed doses, nor does it document the regimen's start date or complete schedule, further limiting assessment.

packet.criterion_text: Worsening cannot be attributed to disease if a confounder (non-adherence, infection, steroid-withdrawal or seasonal flare) is unaddressed.

evidence.quote: doses taken 2026-05-10 and 2026-05-24; doses due 2026-06-07 and 2026-06-21 were missed. Net: two missed doses, i.e., no biologic received in the 5 weeks prior to today.
first dose administered today in clinic after standard loading discussion
Plaques larger and thicker across trunk and lower extremities; nightly itch disrupting sleep most nights.

packet.reasoning: Concession: the non-adherence confounder is present and unaddressed, and this criterion cannot pass. The chart documents that the first biologic dose was administered on 2026-05-10 and that doses 3 and 4 (due 2026-06-07 and 2026-06-21) were missed. The sharpest worsening recorded at visit 4 (PASI 14.2→16.8, DLQI 15→17, plaques larger and thicker) falls entirely within the period of non-adherence. The new evidence retrieved does not contain any dated encounter between 2026-05-10 and 2026-06-07 showing continued deterioration prior to the first missed dose, nor does any chart document explicitly exclude non-adherence as a contributor to the worsening trend. Because the rubric requires that a confounder be either excluded or shown to postdate the worsening, and neither condition is met, disease attribution cannot be established. To settle this question, the chart would need: (a) a dated encounter between 2026-05-10 and 2026-06-07 documenting objective worsening (PASI/BSA/IGA increase) before the first missed dose, or (b) explicit clinician documentation ruling out non-adherence as a driver of the observed deterioration.

challenges.challenge_reason: The packet's own evidence (visit4_adherence_note, 2026-06-29) documents that two consecutive biologic doses were missed and no biologic was received in the 5 weeks prior to the most recent visit — the period during which the sharpest worsening (PASI 14.2→16.8, DLQI 15→17) is recorded. The packet acknowledges this confounder but still assigns status 'worsening' and attributes it to disease activity. The rubric requires that a confounder be either excluded or shown to postdate the worsening before disease attribution is permissible. The packet contains no evidence doing either: it does not show that the PASI/DLQI rise began before the missed doses (2026-06-07 and 2026-06-21), nor does it exclude non-adherence as a driver of the visit-4 deterioration. The packet's own criterion-4 item correctly concedes 'insufficient' for exactly this reason, creating a direct internal inconsistency: criterion 4 acknowledges the gap defeats adequacy assessment, yet criterion 5 proceeds to attribute worsening to disease over the same unresolved gap.
challenges.rubric_quote: A confounder is 'addressed' only if the chart either excludes it or establishes that worsening preceded it. If any confounder is present and unaddressed, this item cannot pass.
challenges.what_would_satisfy: Evidence showing that the objective worsening (PASI rise, BSA increase, IGA step-up) was already documented at or before the first missed dose date (2026-06-07) — i.e., a dated encounter between 2026-05-10 and 2026-06-07 showing continued deterioration — or chart documentation explicitly excluding non-adherence as a contributor to the worsening trend.

attempts: confounder missed doses infection steroid seasonal
